metadata.note: Layer 2 upgrade of Layer 1 mechanism_map — adds NAME + ARTICULATION format and exclusion notes

mechanisms.name: Tour-Validated Performance System
mechanisms.articulation: Multi-layer urethane construction validated by more professional wins than any other ball. The implicit claim: if tour players choose it, it's the best ball for everyone.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: DO NOT position iON+ as a tour ball or claim tour-level spin control. This is Titleist's territory. Instead, reframe: tour optimization is WRONG for your swing speed.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: None — Titleist articulates this extremely well. But the gap is that their articulation is WRONG for 70% of golfers.

mechanisms.name: Direct-to-Consumer Premium
mechanisms.articulation: Same construction as premium balls, sold directly without retail markup, at roughly half the price. The claim: you're paying for the name, not the ball.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: DO NOT lead with DTC/value messaging — Vice owns this frame. iON+ leads with ENGINEERING for specific swing speeds, not 'same ball cheaper.'
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Vice never explains WHY their ball works for any specific golfer. It's always comparative ('like Pro V1 but cheaper'). No mechanism for matching to swing type.

mechanisms.name: Warehouse Value Play
mechanisms.articulation: Premium 3-piece urethane construction at commodity pricing through Costco's distribution model. The claim: why pay more?
mechanisms.exclusion_note: DO NOT compete on price alone. Kirkland owns rock-bottom pricing. iON+ needs to separate from Kirkland by performance credibility.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Large — Kirkland has no performance story. No testing data, no swing-speed matching, no mechanism beyond 'cheap and decent.' Performance complaints are common.

mechanisms.name: Swing-Specific Fitting System
mechanisms.articulation: Proprietary ball fitting algorithm that matches ball model to swing characteristics, delivered through free demo day events.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CAN reference ball fitting concept — but frame iON+ as ALREADY fitted for your swing speed range. No fitting event needed. The engineering IS the fitting.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Bridgestone's fitting is perceived as biased (fitters recommend Bridgestone regardless of data). The CONCEPT of matching ball to swing is right. The EXECUTION is compromised by brand capture.

mechanisms.name: Compression-First Feel System
mechanisms.articulation: Ultra-low compression core engineered for the softest feel in golf. HEX aerodynamics for consistent flight.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CAN claim soft feel but DO NOT make it the lead story. Callaway owns 'softest ball.' iON+ leads with distance.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Callaway connects compression to feel but NOT to distance optimization. No explanation of how compression affects distance for different swing speeds.

mechanisms.name: 5-Layer Performance Architecture
mechanisms.articulation: Only 5-piece construction in golf with dedicated speed, spin, and feel layers.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: DO NOT compete on layer count. More layers ≠ better performance for average swing speeds.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: TM explains the TECHNOLOGY (5 layers) but not the BENEFIT (what it means for your game). Feature-heavy, benefit-light.

mechanisms.name: Precision Engineering with Value
mechanisms.articulation: FastLayer DG core with graduating softness from center to edge. Japanese precision engineering at competitive pricing.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CAN reference core technology concepts. Srixon is respected but not dominant. Differentiate through swing-speed specificity.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Srixon has good technology articulation but weak brand positioning. Known more for sales/deals than for performance leadership.

mechanisms.name: Retail-Owned Premium
mechanisms.articulation: Tour-level urethane construction sold through owned retail at aggressive pricing. No middleman markup.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: Similar to Vice — don't compete on 'same ball cheaper.' Maxfli has no unique mechanism claim.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Large — Maxfli is a strong value product with zero mechanism differentiation. Just 'good and cheap.'

mechanisms.name: Wilson Budget/Value Line
mechanisms.articulation: Affordable golf balls from a trusted American sports brand. Multiple tiers from budget to mid-premium.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: Wilson operates below iON+ price point. Not a direct competitor but represents the 'cheap ball' segment iON+ needs to differentiate from.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Wilson has no performance mechanism story. It's brand trust + price.

mechanisms.name: Tour Player Endorsement
mechanisms.articulation: Tour player X uses this ball, therefore it's the best ball. Endorsement = validation.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: DO NOT use tour endorsement as a mechanism. iON+ is NOT a tour ball and shouldn't pretend to be. Use independent robot testing instead.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Tour endorsement assumes the amateur should play what the pro plays. The OPPOSITE is true for swing speed optimization.

mechanisms.name: Triple Track Alignment System
mechanisms.articulation: Three-line visual alignment aid on the ball for putting accuracy.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: iON+ has SUPERIOR visual alignment technology. Don't ignore Triple Track — acknowledge it, then differentiate. iON+ alignment works for BOTH tee shots and putting. Triple Track is putting only.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Triple Track is positioned as a nice-to-have feature, not a core technology. No one has built a SYSTEM around visual alignment.

mechanisms.name: Compression Matching
mechanisms.articulation: Match your ball's compression to your swing speed. Low compression for slow speeds, high compression for fast speeds.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CAN reference compression matching — it supports the iON+ engineering story. But iON+ goes BEYOND compression to total ball engineering.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: No brand owns this as a positioning. It's generic advice. iON+ can own the specific range (80-95mph) with specific data.

mechanisms.name: Dimple Aerodynamics
mechanisms.articulation: Proprietary dimple patterns reduce drag and optimize flight characteristics.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: Generic technology claim. iON+ should reference aerodynamics only as supporting engineering, not as a lead mechanism.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Every ball has dimples. No brand has differentiated here in a way golfers remember.

mechanisms.name: Snell Design Philosophy
mechanisms.articulation: Designed by a former Titleist VP of ball engineering. The insider who left and built a better ball for less.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: Snell's 'insider' story is compelling but small-scale. iON+ has a different origin story (PG's engineering investment + independent testing).
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Snell has a great founder story but limited marketing reach. Not a primary competitor.

mechanisms.name: Robot/Lab Testing Claims
mechanisms.owner: No single owner (iON+ can OWN this)
mechanisms.articulation: Independent robot testing at certified laboratories to validate performance claims.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: THIS IS iON+'s TERRITORY TO CLAIM. No competitor leads with independent third-party robot testing as their primary proof mechanism. Golf Laboratories data is the credibility anchor.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: MASSIVE — no brand has made independent testing the CENTER of their positioning. Most cite their own internal testing or vague claims. iON+ can own 'independently tested, independently proven.'

mechanisms.name: Launch Monitor Validation
mechanisms.articulation: Personal launch monitor testing (Trackman, GCQuad) as the ultimate arbiter of ball performance.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CAN reference launch monitor data to support claims. The community already trusts this methodology.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: This is consumer behavior, not a brand mechanism. But iON+ can align with it: 'Don't trust our marketing. Test it on your launch monitor.'

mechanisms.name: Multi-Layer Construction (generic)
mechanisms.articulation: More layers = more specialized performance. Each layer serves a different function.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: Don't lead with layer count. Explain what the 3-piece construction DOES for their swing speed.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: Generic claim that every multi-piece ball makes. Needs translation into benefit language.

mechanisms.name: Urethane vs. Ionomer Cover Debate
mechanisms.articulation: Urethane covers provide more greenside spin, ionomer covers are more durable. Urethane = premium, ionomer = budget.
mechanisms.exclusion_note: CRITICAL to reframe. iON+ is ionomer by design, not by compromise. The ionomer cover REDUCES driver spin which INCREASES distance for moderate swing speeds. This is engineering, not cost-cutting.
mechanisms.articulation_gap: The market frames this as a quality hierarchy (urethane > ionomer). Nobody has reframed ionomer as the OPTIMAL choice for most golfers' swing speeds.

ion_plus_unique_mechanism_opportunities: Swing-Speed Optimized Engineering — no competitor owns this specific positioning
Independent Robot Testing as primary proof — Golf Laboratories is credibility gold
Dual-Purpose Visual Alignment System — tee shot AND putting alignment in one design
Ionomer-by-Design — reframe ionomer from 'cheaper' to 'better for YOUR speed'
